purpose: Provides operationally actionable early-warning system for correlation regime flips with ranked leading indicators, lead times, and approximate hit rates
components: Tier 1: IG credit spreads widening >75bp from trough (18-month lead, 6/7 hit rate)
Tier 2a: ISM manufacturing <47 for 2+ consecutive months (12-month lead, 5/6)
Leading indicators: FCI trajectory (is the easing reversing?), repo market stress (approaching reserve scarcity?), AI capex-to-revenue ratio (is the wealth effect sustainable?)
Coincident indicators: core PCE momentum (is transmission reaching consumer prices?), 2Y-SPX correlation sign (has the hedging relationship flipped?)
Structural indicators: Treasury auction metrics (tail sizes, bid-to-cover ratios), Fed QT communication (tapering signals, balance sheet projections)
current_assessment: Leading indicators currently mixed: FCI still easy, repo markets calm, AI capex-to-revenue elevated at 4-6x but returns accruing over 5-10+ year horizons. Coincident indicators show no reconnection. Structural indicators showing stress at margins ($2.7-3.3T annual private absorption). Historical pattern suggests longer broken transmission leads to more violent eventual reconnection.
limitations: Small sample (7 transitions) limits statistical power
Hit rates should be treated as approximate, not precise probabilities
Current fiscal environment may change lead times
Hit rates may not be stable across different macro regimes
Framework is qualitative and relies on subjective threshold judgments for each indicator
Signpost ordering may not hold — structural stress could emerge before leading indicators turn
Historical base rate for broken transmission resolution: 2/4 ended in sharp corrections, but n=4 is insufficient for reliable base rates
The framework does not specify actionable trigger levels for portfolio repositioning
